name: Form
description: EditForm wrapper with styled validation, field groups, and submit state.

api.name: Form
api.description: EditForm wrapper with styled validation, field groups, and submit state.

parameters.name: Model
parameters.description: The form's data model. A JSON snapshot is captured on init (and re-captured whenever a new instance is bound) so ResetValues can restore it later.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The form's fields — typically a sequence of FormFields wrapping bound inputs.

parameters.name: OnValidSubmit
parameters.description: Invoked with Model on submit when validation passes (no Validator, or it reports no errors).

parameters.name: OnInvalidSubmit
parameters.description: Invoked with Model on submit when Validator reports validation errors.

parameters.name: Validator
parameters.description: Optional synchronous validator run on submit and via ValidateField; its errors are merged into the cascaded FormContext alongside any async field-level errors.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the <form> element.

parameters.name: ModelTypeInfo
parameters.description: Optional source-generated JsonTypeInfo{T} for TModel, used for the init/ResetValues snapshot round-trip instead of reflection-based serialization. Without this, the snapshot uses JsonSerializer.Serialize/Deserialize<TModel> via reflection: TModel itself carries [DynamicallyAccessedMembers(All)] so ITS members survive trimming, but that annotation does NOT cascade into the types of TModel's own reference-type properties (e.g. a nested Address POCO) — their members can still be trimmed away, silently dropping nested values on ResetValues. Pass your own JsonSerializerContext-generated MyModelContext.Default.TModel here to cover the whole object graph and be fully trim-safe (#364 review).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the <form> element.

events.name: OnValidSubmit
events.description: Invoked with Model on submit when validation passes (no Validator, or it reports no errors).

events.name: OnInvalidSubmit
events.description: Invoked with Model on submit when Validator reports validation errors.

examples.title: Input with Validation
examples.code:
<FormField Name="email" Label="Email" Required="true" Error="@_emailError" HelpText="We will never share your email.">
    <Input Type="email" Placeholder="you@example.com" @bind-Value="_emailValue" />
</FormField>
<Button OnClick="ValidateEmail">Validate</Button>

@code {
    private string? _emailValue;
    private string? _emailError;

    private void ValidateEmail()
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_emailValue) || !_emailValue.Contains('@'))
            _emailError = "Please enter a valid email address.";
        else
            _emailError = null;
    }
}

examples.title: Select with Validation
examples.code:
<FormField Name="role" Label="Role" Required="true" Error="@_roleError">
    <Select @bind-Value="_roleValue">
        <SelectTrigger>
            <Text As="span" Class="@(_roleValue == null ? \"text-muted-foreground\" : "")">
                @(_roleValue ?? "Select a role")
            </Text>
        </SelectTrigger>
        <SelectContent>
            <SelectItem Value="admin">Admin</SelectItem>
            <SelectItem Value="editor">Editor</SelectItem>
            <SelectItem Value="viewer">Viewer</SelectItem>
        </SelectContent>
    </Select>
</FormField>
<Button OnClick="ValidateRole">Validate</Button>

@code {
    private string? _roleValue;
    private string? _roleError;

    private void ValidateRole()
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(_roleValue))
            _roleError = "Please select a role.";
        else
            _roleError = null;
    }
}

examples.title: Checkbox and Switch with Validation
examples.code:
<FormField Name="terms" Error="@_termsError">
    <Flex Gap="2" Align="center">
        <Checkbox @bind-Checked="_termsChecked" />
        <Label>I accept the terms and conditions</Label>
    </Flex>
</FormField>
<FormField Name="notifications" Label="Email Notifications" HelpText="Receive updates about your account.">
    <Switch @bind-Checked="_notifChecked" />
</FormField>
<Button OnClick="ValidateTerms">Submit</Button>

@code {
    private bool _termsChecked;
    private bool _notifChecked = true;
    private string? _termsError;

    private void ValidateTerms()
    {
        _termsError = _termsChecked ? null : "You must accept the terms to continue.";
    }
}

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<FormField Name="username">
    <FormItem>
        <FormLabel>Username</FormLabel>
        <Input Placeholder="Enter your username" />
        <FormDescription>This is your public display name.</FormDescription>
    </FormItem>
</FormField>

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Helper text describing the field, rendered below its label.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description:
Additional CSS classes merged onto the

.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description:
Unmatched attributes splatted onto the

.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Label text rendered above (or, in Horizontal mode, beside) the control, linked via <label for> to ChildContent's bound input.

parameters.name: HelpText
parameters.description: Helper text shown below the control when there is no active error.

parameters.name: Error
parameters.description: Manually-set error message, used when there's no parent Form context (or no Name) to source live errors from. A live error from the form's validation always takes precedence.

parameters.name: Required
parameters.description: Marks the field as required, rendering a visual asterisk next to Label.

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Layout direction: Vertical (default) stacks label above the control; Horizontal places a right-aligned label beside it in a two-column grid.

parameters.name: AutoRenderMessage
parameters.description: When true (default — the simple-mode API used by the vast majority of consumers), FormField renders the validation error itself below the control. Set false when you compose the field with a child FormMessage that should own the error rendering: this suppresses FormField's own error so the two never render it twice. The error still flows into the cascaded context either way, so a deferred FormMessage can pick it up.

parameters.name: LabelWidth
parameters.description: Fixed CSS width for the label column in Horizontal mode (e.g. "8rem"). Ignored in Vertical mode.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Field key used to look up live validation errors from the parent Form's context, and as the async-validation registration key.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The bound input control(s) this field wraps.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the field's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the field's root element.

parameters.name: AsyncValidator
parameters.description: Optional async validator. Receives the current Value, returns null when valid or an error message string otherwise. The result is stored in Errors against Name.

parameters.name: AsyncValidationDebounceMs
parameters.description: Debounce window in ms. 0 disables debouncing.

parameters.name: AsyncValidateOn
parameters.description: When async validation runs. Defaults to OnBlur.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Current value of the bound input. Required for OnChange auto-triggering; also passed to the validator on blur. If unset, consumers may invoke ValidateAsync(object?) directly.

parameters.name: OnValidatingChanged
parameters.description: Fired whenever IsValidating flips.

records.name: FormFieldContext
records.signature: FormFieldContext(string? Name, string? Error, bool IsValidating, string ControlId = "", string? DescribedBy = null, bool AutoRenderMessage = true, string? ErrorId = null, string? LabelId = null)

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The item's content — typically a label, control, and description/message grouped as one field unit.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root element.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The label's text content.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the <label>.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the <label>.

Additional CSS classes merged onto the rendered error

mdSummary:
# Form

EditForm wrapper with styled validation, field groups, and submit state.

**Package:** `Lumeo` · **Category:** Forms/Form Composition
**Docs:** https://lumeo.nativ.sh/components/form

## Parameters
| Owner | Name | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Form | Model | `TModel?` | `` | The form's data model. A JSON snapshot is captured on init (and re-captured whenever a new instance is bound) so ResetValues can restore it later. |
| Form | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The form's fields — typically a sequence of FormFields wrapping bound inputs. |
| Form | OnValidSubmit | `EventCallback<TModel>` | `` | Invoked with Model on submit when validation passes (no Validator, or it reports no errors). |
| Form | OnInvalidSubmit | `EventCallback<TModel>` | `` | Invoked with Model on submit when Validator reports validation errors. |
| Form | Validator | `IFormValidator?` | `` | Optional synchronous validator run on submit and via ValidateField; its errors are merged into the cascaded FormContext alongside any async field-level errors. |
| Form | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the \<form\> element. |
| Form | ModelTypeInfo | `JsonTypeInfo<TModel>?` | `` | Optional source-generated JsonTypeInfo{T} for TModel, used for the init/ResetValues snapshot round-trip instead of reflection-based serialization. Without this, the snapshot uses JsonSerializer.Serialize/Deserialize\<TModel\> via reflection: TModel itself carries [DynamicallyAccessedMembers(All)] so ITS members survive trimming, but that annotation does NOT cascade into the types of TModel's own reference-type properties (e.g. a nested Address POCO) — their members can still be trimmed away, silently dropping nested values on ResetValues. Pass your own JsonSerializerContext-generated MyModelContext.Default.TModel here to cover the whole object graph and be fully trim-safe (#364 review). |
| Form | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Unmatched attributes splatted onto the \<form\> element. |
| FormDescription | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | Helper text describing the field, rendered below its label. |
| FormDescription | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the \<p\>. |
| FormDescription | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Unmatched attributes splatted onto the \<p\>. |
| FormField | Label | `string?` | `` | Label text rendered above (or, in Horizontal mode, beside) the control, linked via \<label for\> to ChildContent's bound input. |
| FormField | HelpText | `string?` | `` | Helper text shown below the control when there is no active error. |
| FormField | Error | `string?` | `` | Manually-set error message, used when there's no parent Form context (or no Name) to source live errors from. A live error from the form's validation always takes precedence. |
| FormField | Required | `bool` | `false` | Marks the field as required, rendering a visual asterisk next to Label. |
| FormField | Orientation | `Lumeo.Orientation` | `Lumeo.Orientation.Vertical` | Layout direction: Vertical (default) stacks label above the control; Horizontal places a right-aligned label beside it in a two-column grid. |
| FormField | AutoRenderMessage | `bool` | `true` | When true (default — the simple-mode API used by the vast majority of consumers), FormField renders the validation error itself below the control. Set false when you compose the field with a child FormMessage that should own the error rendering: this suppresses FormField's own error so the two never render it twice. The error still flows into the cascaded context either way, so a deferred FormMessage can pick it up. |
| FormField | LabelWidth | `string?` | `` | Fixed CSS width for the label column in Horizontal mode (e.g. "8rem"). Ignored in Vertical mode. |
| FormField | Name | `string?` | `` | Field key used to look up live validation errors from the parent Form's context, and as the async-validation registration key. |
| FormField | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The bound input control(s) this field wraps. |
| FormField | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the field's root element. |
| FormField | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Unmatched attributes splatted onto the field's root element. |
| FormField | AsyncValidator | `Func<object?, Task<string?>>?` | `` | Optional async validator. Receives the current Value, returns null when valid or an error message string otherwise. The result is stored in Errors against Name. |
| FormField | AsyncValidationDebounceMs | `int` | `0` | Debounce window in ms. 0 disables debouncing. |
| FormField | AsyncValidateOn | `AsyncValidationTrigger` | `AsyncValidationTrigger.OnBlur` | When async validation runs. Defaults to OnBlur. |
| FormField | Value | `object?` | `` | Current value of the bound input. Required for OnChange auto-triggering; also passed to the validator on blur. If unset, consumers may invoke ValidateAsync(object?) directly. |
| FormField | OnValidatingChanged | `EventCallback<bool>` | `` | Fired whenever IsValidating flips. |
| FormField | ParentFormContext | `FormContext?` | `` |  |
| FormItem | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The item's content — typically a label, control, and description/message grouped as one field unit. |
| FormItem | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the root element. |
| FormItem | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Unmatched attributes splatted onto the root element. |
| FormLabel | FieldContext | `FormField.FormFieldContext?` | `` |  |
| FormLabel | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The label's text content. |
| FormLabel | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the \<label\>. |
| FormLabel | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Unmatched attributes splatted onto the \<label\>. |
| FormMessage | FieldContext | `FormField.FormFieldContext?` | `` |  |
| FormMessage | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the rendered error \<p\>. |
| FormMessage | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Unmatched attributes splatted onto the rendered error \<p\>. |

## Slots
- `ChildContent` (Form) — The form's fields — typically a sequence of FormFields wrapping bound inputs.
- `ChildContent` (FormDescription) — Helper text describing the field, rendered below its label.
- `ChildContent` (FormField) — The bound input control(s) this field wraps.
- `ChildContent` (FormItem) — The item's content — typically a label, control, and description/message grouped as one field unit.
- `ChildContent` (FormLabel) — The label's text content.

## Events
- `OnValidSubmit` (`EventCallback<TModel>`) — Invoked with Model on submit when validation passes (no Validator, or it reports no errors).
- `OnInvalidSubmit` (`EventCallback<TModel>`) — Invoked with Model on submit when Validator reports validation errors.

## Sub-components
- `FormDescription`, `FormField`, `FormItem`, `FormLabel`, `FormMessage`

## Cascading values it expects
- `ParentFormContext` (`FormContext?`)
- `FieldContext` (`FormField.FormFieldContext?`)

## Services it injects
- (none)

## Keyboard
—

## Example
```razor
<Form />
```

## CSS variables
`--color-destructive`, `--color-muted`, `--color-muted-foreground`

## Files
- `UI/Form/DataAnnotationsFormValidator.cs`
- `UI/Form/Form.razor`
- `UI/Form/Form.razor.cs`
- `UI/Form/FormContext.cs`
- `UI/Form/FormDescription.razor`
- `UI/Form/FormField.razor`
- `UI/Form/FormItem.razor`
- `UI/Form/FormLabel.razor`
- `UI/Form/FormMessage.razor`
- `UI/Form/IFormValidator.cs`
